Scam guides

Browse all published guides by scam type, payment method, platform, or impersonation pattern.

marketplace

Facebook Marketplace Scams: How to Protect Yourself in the UK

Facebook Marketplace has become a popular platform for buying and selling items in the UK, but scammers are increasingly targeting unsuspecting users. This guide covers the most common scams, warning signs to watch for, and practical steps you can take to protect yourself when using the platform.

Updated 2026-04-20

marketplace

Gumtree Scams in the UK: How to Spot and Avoid Them

Gumtree remains one of the UK's largest online marketplaces, but scammers frequently target buyers and sellers. This guide covers common fraud tactics including fake listings, payment schemes, overpayment tricks, and advance-fee scams. Discover practical steps to verify sellers, secure transactions, and report suspicious activity to stay safe.

Updated 2026-04-20

sms

Evri Text Message Scam: How to Spot and Avoid It

Evri text scams trick UK residents with fake delivery notifications claiming packages need attention. These messages contain malicious links designed to steal personal and financial information. This guide explains how to recognise the scam, what to do if you receive one, and how to report it to authorities.

Updated 2026-04-20

website

Is Temu a Scam? What UK Consumers Need to Know

Temu is a Chinese-owned e-commerce platform that has gained millions of UK users by offering extremely cheap products. While not technically a scam, the platform raises legitimate concerns about data privacy, product quality, and manufacturing standards that consumers should consider.

Updated 2026-04-17

website

Is SHEIN Legit or a Scam? What UK Shoppers Need to Know

SHEIN operates as a legal business, but shoppers in the UK face real risks including delays of 2-4 weeks, inconsistent product quality, unexpected import fees, and limited consumer protections. This guide explains what to expect, how to spot problems, and your rights when ordering from SHEIN to the UK.

Updated 2026-04-17

website

Is AliExpress Safe? A UK Buyer's Guide to Avoiding Scams

AliExpress is a genuine Chinese marketplace, but it operates differently from UK retailers. This guide explains genuine risks UK customers face, red flags to watch for, how the platform's buyer protection works, and practical steps to shop more safely on AliExpress.

Updated 2026-04-17

marketplace

Vinted Scam Warning: How to Stay Safe on the UK Marketplace

A comprehensive guide to common Vinted scams affecting UK users, including advance payment schemes, counterfeit goods, and non-delivery fraud. Discover practical steps to verify sellers, use secure payment methods, and report suspicious activity to keep your transactions safe.

Updated 2026-04-17

marketplace

Facebook Marketplace Scams: How to Stay Safe When Buying and Selling in the UK

Facebook Marketplace has become a popular platform for buying and selling items in the UK, but scammers are increasingly targeting users through fake listings, payment manipulation, and phishing tactics. This guide explains the most common scams, warning signs to watch for, and practical steps to protect yourself when using the platform.

Updated 2026-04-17

marketplace

Gumtree Scams UK: How to Spot and Avoid Common Fraud Risks

Gumtree is one of the UK's largest classifieds platforms, but it attracts scammers targeting both buyers and sellers. This guide covers the most prevalent Gumtree scams, red flags to watch for, safe payment methods, and steps to take if you suspect fraudulent activity. Follow our advice to buy and sell safely.

Updated 2026-04-17

marketplace

eBay Buyer Protection in the UK: What You Need to Know to Stay Safe

This guide explains eBay's buyer protection programme for UK customers, including how to file claims for non-delivery and item not as described cases. Discover red flags to watch for, best practices when purchasing, and what to do if you fall victim to a scam on the platform.

Updated 2026-04-17

sms

Royal Mail Text Scam: How to Spot and Avoid Fraudulent SMS Messages

Royal Mail text scams are increasingly common in the UK, with criminals sending fake SMS messages claiming delivery issues or requiring payment. These messages contain malicious links designed to steal banking details, addresses, and personal information. This guide explains how to recognise these scams, what to do if you receive one, and how to report fraudulent messages to Royal Mail and authorities.

Updated 2026-04-17

sms

Evri Text Scam: How to Spot and Avoid Fake Delivery Messages

Evri text scams trick UK residents into clicking malicious links by posing as delivery notifications. These fraudulent SMS messages often claim a package failed to deliver and direct victims to fake websites designed to steal personal and financial information. This guide explains the warning signs, what legitimate Evri messages look like, and the steps you should take if you receive a suspicious text.

Updated 2026-04-17

sms

DPD Delivery Scam Text Messages: How to Spot and Avoid Them

DPD delivery scam texts are a common fraud tactic in the UK where criminals send SMS messages pretending to be from the parcel delivery company. These messages typically claim a delivery failed or ask you to confirm details, urging you to click a link. Clicking these links can lead to credential theft, malware installation, or financial fraud. This guide explains how to recognise fake DPD texts, what to do if you receive one, and how to stay safe when expecting deliveries.

Updated 2026-04-17

sms

Yodel Scam Text Messages: How to Spot and Avoid Them

Yodel scam text messages trick UK consumers into clicking malicious links by posing as delivery notifications. These SMS messages typically claim your parcel is pending delivery or requires action, directing you to fake websites designed to harvest login credentials, card details, and personal data. This guide explains the warning signs of fraudulent Yodel texts and the steps you should take if you receive one.

Updated 2026-04-17

sms

UPS Delivery Scam Text Messages: How to Spot and Avoid Them in the UK

UPS delivery scam texts are designed to trick you into clicking malicious links or providing personal information. This guide explains how these scams work, what warning signs to look for, and the steps you should take if you receive a suspicious message claiming to be from UPS.

Updated 2026-04-17

payment

PayPal Email Scams: How to Spot and Avoid Them in the UK

Scammers regularly impersonate PayPal in phishing emails designed to steal login credentials and personal information from UK customers. This guide explains the warning signs of PayPal email scams, how fraudsters operate, and the steps you should take if you receive a suspicious message claiming to be from PayPal.

Updated 2026-04-17

payment

Bank Transfer Scams in the UK: How to Protect Yourself

Bank transfer scams are increasingly sophisticated in the UK, targeting individuals and businesses through impersonation, fake invoices, and pressure tactics. This guide explains common schemes, red flags to watch for, and essential protection measures including verification protocols and reporting procedures.

Updated 2026-04-17

payment

Invoice Scam Emails: How to Protect Your Business from Fraudulent Invoices

Invoice scam emails trick businesses into paying fraudulent invoices by impersonating trusted suppliers or vendors. Scammers intercept genuine purchase orders, create fake invoices with altered bank details, or spoof email addresses to redirect payments. This guide explains common tactics used in UK invoice fraud, practical steps to verify authenticity, and procedures to implement to prevent costly mistakes.

Updated 2026-04-17

payment

Refund Scams in the UK: How to Protect Yourself from Payment Fraud

Refund scams are a growing threat in the UK where fraudsters impersonate businesses, tax authorities, or payment providers to steal money and personal information. Scammers typically contact victims via email, phone, or text claiming an overpayment or tax rebate, then direct them to fake websites or request upfront fees. This guide explains how these scams work, warning signs to watch for, and practical steps to protect yourself and report suspected fraud to Action Fraud.

Updated 2026-04-17

payment

Chargeback Scams: How Fraudsters Exploit Card Payment Disputes in the UK

Chargeback scams exploit the consumer protection mechanisms built into card payment systems. Fraudsters purchase items, receive goods or services, then dispute the charge with their bank claiming the transaction was unauthorised or the goods never arrived. UK businesses lose millions annually to this type of fraud. Understanding how these scams work and implementing verification procedures can significantly reduce your risk.

Updated 2026-04-17

payment

Direct Debit Scams in the UK: How to Protect Your Bank Account

Direct debit fraud is a growing threat in the UK where scammers trick customers into authorizing unauthorized payments from their bank accounts. This guide explains common tactics used by fraudsters, warning signs to watch for, how to cancel fraudulent direct debits, and your rights under the Direct Debit Guarantee scheme. Discover practical steps to verify legitimacy before authorizing any direct debit and how to report suspicious activity to your bank and the authorities.

Updated 2026-04-17

government

HMRC Tax Refund Scams: How to Spot and Avoid Them

HMRC tax refund scams are increasingly common in the UK. Fraudsters impersonate HMRC staff via email, text, or phone calls claiming you are owed a refund. This guide explains how these scams work, red flags to watch for, and how to report suspicious contact claiming to be from HMRC.

Updated 2026-04-17

government

DVLA Scam Emails: How to Spot and Avoid Them

DVLA scam emails are designed to steal your personal information and money by impersonating the UK's Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency. This guide explains common tactics used by scammers, warning signs to watch for, and steps to take if you receive a suspicious email claiming to be from DVLA.

Updated 2026-04-17

government

NHS COVID Scam Messages: How to Spot and Report Them

Scammers send fake SMS, email, and social media messages claiming to be from the NHS about COVID-19 vaccines, test results, or health updates. These messages often contain malicious links or request personal information. The real NHS will never ask for passwords, bank details, or payment via unsolicited messages. Always verify communications by contacting the NHS directly through official channels.

Updated 2026-04-17

crypto

Crypto Investment Scams in the UK: How to Protect Yourself

A practical guide for UK consumers on recognizing and avoiding cryptocurrency investment scams. Covers common tactics used by fraudsters, red flags to watch for, legitimate vs. fraudulent schemes, and steps to take if you have been targeted or scammed.

Updated 2026-04-17

crypto

Bitcoin Scams in the UK: How to Protect Yourself from Crypto Fraud

This guide explores the major Bitcoin scams affecting UK consumers, from pump-and-dump schemes to romance fraud involving cryptocurrency. It covers warning signs, prevention strategies, and guidance on reporting fraud to the FCA and Action Fraud, helping you safeguard your digital assets.

Updated 2026-04-17

crypto

Forex Trading Scams in the UK: How to Protect Yourself

This guide explains how forex trading scams operate in the UK, identifies red flags such as unregulated brokers and unrealistic profit promises, and provides practical steps to verify a broker's FCA authorisation. It covers common tactics used by scammers, including social engineering and pressure sales, and advises readers on reporting fraud and recovering funds.

Updated 2026-04-17

crypto

Trading Signal Scams: How UK Investors Are Being Targeted

Trading signal scams target UK cryptocurrency investors with false promises of profitable trading tips delivered via Telegram, WhatsApp or exclusive websites. Scammers claim insider knowledge or advanced algorithms can predict market movements, charging subscription fees or requesting deposits that are never recovered. This guide explains how these schemes operate, common tactics used by fraudsters, and practical steps UK investors can take to protect themselves.

Updated 2026-04-17

employment

Job Offer Scams in the UK: How to Spot and Avoid Them

Job offer scams deceive UK applicants by posing as legitimate employers, often requesting upfront payments for training, equipment, or background checks. This guide explains common scam tactics, warning signs to watch for, and practical steps to verify job opportunities before committing time or money.

Updated 2026-04-17

employment

Work From Home Scams in the UK: How to Spot and Avoid Them

Work from home opportunities attract millions of UK job seekers, but scammers exploit this demand with fake job postings and fraudulent schemes. This guide explains the most common work from home scams, red flags to watch for, how scammers operate, and practical steps to verify legitimate opportunities before applying or paying money.

Updated 2026-04-17

employment

LinkedIn Job Scams: How to Spot and Avoid Them in the UK

A comprehensive guide to understanding LinkedIn job scams targeting UK job seekers. This article explains common tactics used by scammers, warning signs to watch for, steps to verify legitimate opportunities, and how to report fraudulent activity to LinkedIn and relevant authorities.

Updated 2026-04-17

dating

Romance Scam Signs: How to Protect Yourself on UK Dating Apps

Romance scams cost UK victims thousands annually. This guide outlines common warning signs such as quick declarations of love, requests for money, reluctance to video call, and inconsistent personal details. Learn how to verify profiles, protect your information, and report suspicious activity on dating apps.

Updated 2026-04-17

dating

Tinder Scams in the UK: How to Protect Yourself from Romance and Financial Fraud

A comprehensive guide to understanding common Tinder scams targeting UK users, including romance fraud, catfishing, fake investment schemes, and payment fraud. Discover practical tips to verify profiles, spot warning signs, and report suspicious activity to stay safe while online dating.

Updated 2026-04-17

marketplace

Puppy Scams in the UK: How to Spot and Avoid Fake Breeders

Puppy scams have become increasingly common in the UK, with fraudsters using fake listings, stolen photos, and emotionally manipulative tactics to deceive buyers. This guide explains the warning signs of puppy scams, how scammers operate, steps to verify legitimate breeders, and what to do if you have been targeted. Protecting yourself involves meeting puppies in person, checking health certificates, and using secure payment methods.

Updated 2026-04-17

marketplace

UK Ticket Scams: How to Spot and Avoid Fraudulent Event Tickets

Ticket scams are increasingly common in the UK, targeting fans eager to purchase concert, festival, and sports event tickets. Scammers use counterfeit tickets, phishing websites, and social media impersonation to defraud buyers. This guide explains the warning signs of ticket fraud, safe purchasing practices, and what to do if you have been scammed, including reporting to Action Fraud and your payment provider.

Updated 2026-04-17

travel

Holiday Booking Scams in the UK: How to Protect Yourself

Holiday booking scams targeting UK travellers are increasingly sophisticated, with criminals creating convincing fake websites and posing as legitimate travel agents. This guide covers common scam tactics, red flags to watch for, verification steps before booking, and what to do if you suspect fraud. Protect your money and personal data by understanding how these scams work and following practical safety measures.

Updated 2026-04-17

shopping

How to Spot and Avoid Amazon Scam Emails in the UK

Amazon scam emails are increasingly common in the UK, with fraudsters impersonating the retailer to steal login credentials and payment information. This guide explains what these scams look like, how to spot the red flags, and what steps you should take if you receive a suspicious email claiming to be from Amazon.

Updated 2026-04-17

phone

Amazon Phone Call Scam: How to Protect Yourself in the UK

Amazon phone call scams are increasingly common in the UK, with fraudsters posing as Amazon customer service representatives. These calls often claim there is suspicious activity on your account or an urgent security issue requiring immediate action. Scammers use high-pressure tactics to trick victims into revealing passwords, payment details, or granting remote access to their devices. This guide explains the warning signs of these scams and provides practical steps to stay safe.

Updated 2026-04-17

payment

Amazon Refund Scam: How to Protect Yourself in the UK

Amazon refund scams are increasingly targeting UK customers through fake emails and phone calls claiming unauthorised purchases or account issues. Fraudsters use urgency and fear to manipulate victims into clicking malicious links or providing sensitive information like passwords and payment details. This guide explains how these scams work, the red flags to watch for, and the steps you should take if you suspect you have been targeted.

Updated 2026-04-17

tech

Google Voice Scam UK: How Fraudsters Exploit the Service and How to Stay Safe

Google Voice scams exploit the service's free calling and messaging features to conduct identity fraud, account takeovers, and financial crimes while remaining anonymous. This guide explains how the scam works, red flags to watch for, and what to do if you suspect you are targeted.

Updated 2026-04-17

tech

Microsoft Support Scam: How to Protect Yourself in the UK

Scammers impersonate Microsoft support to trick users into providing remote access or payment details. This guide explains the common tactics used in Microsoft support scams, how to spot warning signs, and the steps to take if you've been targeted or compromised.

Updated 2026-04-17

tech

Apple ID Scam Emails: How to Spot and Avoid Them in the UK

Apple ID scam emails are a common threat in the UK. Scammers impersonate Apple to steal your login credentials and personal information. This guide explains how to recognise these phishing attempts, verify legitimate Apple communications, and take action if you've been targeted. Protect your account by understanding the warning signs and knowing when to contact Apple directly.

Updated 2026-04-17

social

WhatsApp Family Scams: How to Protect Yourself from 'Urgent Money' Messages

WhatsApp family scams involve fraudsters impersonating relatives or close contacts, claiming to be in urgent financial distress. The scammer requests money via bank transfer, often targeting older adults. This guide explains the warning signs, verification steps, and how to report suspicious messages to stay safe.

Updated 2026-04-17

social

Instagram Scam Messages: How to Spot and Avoid Them in the UK

Instagram scam messages are increasingly common in the UK, using tactics like fake verification badges, lottery prizes, and urgent account warnings to deceive users. This guide explains the most prevalent scams, how to recognise them, and the steps you should take if you receive a suspicious message.

Updated 2026-04-17

social

Snapchat Scam Accounts: How to Spot and Avoid Them in the UK

Snapchat scam accounts pose a growing threat to UK users. This guide covers the red flags of fake accounts, common scams like romance and job offer frauds, credential theft attempts, and actionable steps to verify genuine accounts, report suspicious activity, and secure your Snapchat profile.

Updated 2026-04-17

tech

QR Code Payment Scams in the UK: How to Stay Safe

Criminals are exploiting QR codes to redirect payments to fake accounts and compromise personal devices. This guide explains the risks, common tactics, and practical steps UK consumers should take to avoid QR code payment fraud.

Updated 2026-04-17

sms

Parking Fine Scam Text Messages: How to Spot and Avoid Them

Parking fine scam texts are becoming increasingly common in the UK, with cybercriminals impersonating legitimate parking operators to trick victims into paying fake penalties. These SMS messages typically claim you have an outstanding parking violation and demand urgent payment via a suspicious link. This guide explains how to recognise these scams, verify genuine parking fines, and report fraudulent messages to protect your personal and financial information.

Updated 2026-04-17

utility

Energy Bill Scams in the UK: How to Spot and Avoid Them

This guide explains the most common energy bill scams affecting UK consumers, including unsolicited contact from fake suppliers, pressure to switch providers, inflated meter readings, and phishing emails. Discover practical steps to verify genuine communications, protect your personal data, and report suspicious activity to Ofgem and Action Fraud.

Updated 2026-04-17

finance

Credit Score Scams in the UK: How to Protect Yourself

Credit score scams target UK consumers with false promises of improved credit ratings for upfront fees. This guide explains common tactics used by scammers, how to identify fraudulent credit repair services, and what legitimate options exist for improving your credit score through official channels like Experian, Equifax, and Clearscore.

Updated 2026-04-17

finance

Debt Relief Scams in the UK: How to Spot and Avoid Them

Debt relief scams are increasingly targeting financially struggling UK residents with unrealistic promises. This guide explains how these scams operate, what warning signs to look for, and how to access genuine debt advice from FCA-regulated providers and free government services.
